faults.location: 3.4.1 Signals from the Central Module to the Taxi module
faults.description: When the driver submits an order and no taxi is available (Variant 4b), the central must inform the taxi that the order was rejected so a new order can be submitted. No signal exists in 3.4.1 to communicate order rejection back to the taxi for driver-submitted orders.

faults.location: 3.1 Global Definitions and Data structures (Order_struct)
faults.description: UC 1.2 Task 2 requires that orders can be prioritized (some customers are more important than others), but Order_struct contains no priority field. The Misc field is for miscellaneous text and cannot serve as a typed priority indicator.

faults.location: 3.3.2 Signals from the central module to the user (operator)
faults.description: Variant 3b requires that if the order is very close in time, the system presents an estimate of how long it takes for a car to be available. No signal in 3.3.2 carries an estimated wait-time or availability estimate to the operator.

faults.location: 3.2.1 Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: LoginSignal has no parameters, yet UC 1.4 Task 2-3 requires that driver identification information is sent to the central. Without driver ID in the signal, the central cannot identify which driver is logging in.

faults.location: 3.4.2 Signals from the Taxi Module to the Central Module
faults.description: LoggedIn(integer) carries only the taxi number. UC 1.4 Task 3 requires that driver information is sent to the central upon login, but no driver number or driver identity is included in this signal.

faults.location: 3.4.1 Signals from the Central Module to the Taxi module
faults.description: UC 1.4 Variant 4b requires the central to reject an invalid driver card and keep the car in Offline state. Section 3.4.1 defines LoginOk but contains no LoginFailed signal from central to taxi to communicate login rejection.

faults.location: 3.2.2 Signals from the taxi module to the user (taxi driver)
faults.description: UC 1.5 Task 4 requires that the central confirms the order to the driver after the driver accepts it. No Confirm signal is defined in 3.2.2 (taxi module to driver), so the driver is never notified of the central's confirmation.

faults.location: 3.4.1 Signals from the Central Module to the Taxi module
faults.description: Variant 3b requires that if the order is not accepted within 2 minutes, it is automatically cancelled. No signal exists in 3.4.1 to inform the taxi that the order was auto-cancelled, leaving the driver without notification of cancellation.

faults.location: 3.4.2 Signals from the Taxi Module to the Central Module
faults.description: UC 1.6 Task 8 requires the driver to send arrival zone AND estimated time of arrival to the central when entering state Soon Available. Position(integer, integer) carries only taxi number and zone number; no parameter exists for the arrival time.

faults.location: 3.4.2 Signals from the Taxi Module to the Central Module
faults.description: UC 1.7 Task 1 and UC 1.8 Task 1-2 require that a driver alarm notifies the central and opens a voice channel. No Alarm signal is defined in 3.4.2 for the taxi to notify the central that the alarm has been triggered.

faults.location: 3.2.1 Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: UC 1.8 Task 1 requires the driver to trigger an alarm by pressing the alarm button. No Alarm signal is defined in 3.2.1 for the driver to initiate the alarm in the taxi module.

faults.location: 3.4.2 Signals from the Taxi Module to the Central Module
faults.description: UC 1.8 Task 3 requires exact coordinates (GPS position) sent to the central every 30 seconds during alarm. The only positioning signal Position(integer, integer) carries a zone number (an integer representing an area), not exact coordinates. Exact coordinates cannot be transmitted with the existing signal.

faults.location: 3.2.1 Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: UC 1.7 Task 3 and UC 1.8 Task 4 specify that only the operator can reset the alarm, not the driver. However, Reset_Alarm(integer) is defined in 3.2.1 as a user-to-taxi signal, meaning the driver can reset the alarm from the terminal, which contradicts the UC requirement.

faults.location: 3.4.2 Signals from the Taxi Module to the Central Module
faults.description: UC 1.11 Task 4 requires each car receiving a text message to automatically acknowledge reception to the central. No text message acknowledgment signal is defined in 3.4.2 (taxi to central).

faults.location: 3.3.2 Signals from the central module to the user (operator)
faults.description: UC 1.11 Task 5 and Variants 4b/4c require the system to confirm delivery of text messages and notify the operator of delivery failure. No delivery confirmation or failure notification signal for text messages is defined in 3.3.2.

faults.location: 3.3.1 Signals from the user (operator) to the central module
faults.description: Taxi(integer, integer, integer) is placed in 3.3.1 (operator-to-central direction) but its description states 'this is the response' — a response signal belongs in 3.3.2 (central-to-operator). The signal direction is wrong.

faults.location: 3.2.1 Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: UC 1.16 Task 1 requires the driver to request car and driver properties via the terminal. No signal is defined in 3.2.1 for the driver to request property information.

faults.location: 3.4.1 Signals from the Central Module to the Taxi module
faults.description: UC 1.16 Task 2 requires the central to send car and driver property information to the taxi terminal. No signal carrying property information is defined in 3.4.1.

faults.location: 3.3.1 Signals from the user (operator) to the central module
faults.description: UC 1.18 Tasks 2-3 require the operator to select a driver and update their properties. No signal for updating driver properties is defined in 3.3.1.

faults.location: 3.3.2 Signals from the central module to the user (operator)
faults.description: UC 1.18 Task 4 requires the system to acknowledge the property update to the operator. No acknowledgment signal for driver property updates is defined in 3.3.2.

faults.location: 3.6 Communication Protocol (Table 1)
faults.description: Confirm(integer, integer) is defined in 3.4.1 as a signal sent from the central to a taxi to confirm a submitted order, and it appears in MSC 4.1. However, Confirm is not listed as a message type in Table 1, making it impossible to transmit over the communication link.

faults.location: 4.1 MSC Order handling, normal case
faults.description: MSC 4.1 Step 1 shows the operator sending Order(New_Order) to the central, but section 3.3.1 defines the operator-to-central order signal as OrderOper(Order_struct, integer). The MSC uses the wrong signal name, inconsistent with the API definition.

faults.location: 4.1 MSC Order handling, normal case
faults.description: UC 1.5 Task 4 requires the central to confirm the order back to the taxi after receiving the Ack. MSC 4.1 shows no Confirm signal from Central to Taxi/Driver after step 8 (Ack reaches Central), omitting a required UC step.

faults.location: 4.2 MSC Voice communication, central initiated
faults.description: MSC 4.2 Steps 2-3 show Start_Voice(Operator_Nbr, Taxi_Nbr) transmitted from Central to Comm_link and from Comm_link to Taxi. However, 3.4.1 defines Start_Voice(integer, integer) with parameters 'taxi number and operator number' (taxi first, operator second), so the parameter order in the MSC is reversed.

faults.location: 3.2.1 Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: MSC 4.2 Step 5 shows Driver→Taxi: Confirm_Voice(Taxi_Nbr), but no Confirm_Voice signal is defined in 3.2.1 (user-to-taxi signals). The driver has no defined signal to confirm voice connection setup to the taxi module.

faults.location: 4.2 MSC Voice communication, central initiated
faults.description: MSC 4.2 Steps 10-11 show Voice_Msg(Operator_Nbr, Taxi_Nbr, Voice_Data) sent Central→Comm_link and Comm_link→Taxi. Section 3.4.1 defines Voice_msg(integer, integer, integer) with parameters 'taxi number, operator number and voice data' (taxi first), so the parameter order in the MSC is reversed relative to the signal definition.

faults.location: 4.2 MSC Voice communication, central initiated
faults.description: MSC 4.2 Step 15 shows Driver→Taxi: Start_Voice(Taxi_Nbr) with only one parameter. Section 3.2.1 defines Start_Voice(integer, integer) with two parameters: sending taxi number and operator number. The second parameter (operator number) is missing in the MSC representation.
